subject: Anthropology
title: Primate Classification
description: A fast breakdown of Primate Classification for builders.

hook.text: Lemurs, monkeys, and apes look different but share a common ancestor. How do scientists sort the family tree?

buildup.text: Primates split into two suborders: Strepsirrhini (lemurs, lorises) and Haplorhini (tarsiers, monkeys, apes).

discovery.text: Apes (hominoids) lack tails and have larger brains. Humans belong to the great apes alongside chimps and gorillas.

twist.text: Humans aren't descended from chimps. Both species share a common ancestor that lived about seven million years ago.

climax.text: Classification reveals relationships. Knowing where we sit on the tree shows how evolution shaped our lineage.

punchline.text: We don't come from apes — we are apes.

quiz.question: To which group do humans belong?
options: Great apes alongside chimps and gorillas
